toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
This is an MSTest project using Microsoft Testing Platform (MTP) based on `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true`. The user wants to run tests and collect crash dumps if the test host crashes.

For .NET tests with Microsoft Testing Platform, I can use the `--crashdump` option (or `--crash-dump`) to collect crash dumps when the test host crashes. Let me invoke the run-tests skill since it has specialized knowledge about test platforms.

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-435fb2cce8af4a84be3e465421824307 && dotnet test --crashdump 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run tests with crash dump collection

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump via MTP passthrough

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with crash dump via MTP passthrough

data.reasoningText:
The `--crashdump` switch isn't recognized by `dotnet test` in this context. Since this project uses Microsoft Testing Platform (MTP) via `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, the crash dump option might need to be passed differently. Let me check the correct approach.

For MTP, the options are passed as `-- --crashdump` to forward them to the test host. Let me try that.

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump via MTP passthrough

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-435fb2cce8af4a84be3e465421824307 && dotnet test -- --crashdump 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with crash dump via MTP passthrough", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run tests with crash dump via MTP passthrough
